Lab Guide › Working in the Lab

Pay for things

How to purchase lab items and get reimbursed — preferred routes and approvals.

Getting Started

  • Start guide: Undergraduates
  • Start guide: Graduate students
  • Start guide: Postdocs

Working in the Lab

  • Work expectations
  • Time off & vacation
  • Support resources
  • Be safe
  • Pay for things
  • Travel
  • Ship packages
  • Lab operations roles

Research Workflow

  • Develop a research hypothesis
  • Review the literature
  • Develop quarterly SMART goals
  • Manage data
  • Code
  • Analyze data
  • Analyze error & uncertainty

  • Write a first draft
  • Design your figures
  • Paper revisions
  • Review papers
  • Make a poster

  • Communicating in the lab
  • Discussion guidelines
  • Meetings

Professional Development

  • Attending a conference
  • Applying for funding
  • Request recommendation letters
  • File your dissertation

You must have Christina’s written permission before purchasing anything for the lab. If you use OPP this is built into the process; if you buy out of pocket you need permission both to make the purchase and for what the purchase is.

For travel or conference expenses, see Travel instead.

Preferred order of routes

  1. Amazon (when possible) — fastest, easiest tracking.
  2. OPP / pre-purchasing — for non-Amazon vendors or special items.
  3. UC Davis Stores — for items they carry.
  4. Out-of-pocket — only when approved in advance (rare).

Amazon orders

Order through Amazon via AggieEnterprise (Christina only) — don’t order it yourself. Submit a request through Slack (Purchase Hub → “Request Amazon Purchase”). Include exact color/model/size in the item name and justification to avoid mis-orders.

OPP (pre-purchasing) for non-Amazon items

Use when an item isn’t on Amazon or must come from another vendor. First, ask Christina to add you to the correct work group. Then submit a request via Slack (Purchase Hub → “Pre-Purchasing Request”), complete the fields in pre-purchasing, and submit a separate request per vendor. It routes to Christina for approval.

Include the URL and any special item codes or offers, otherwise the purchaser will just buy at full price.

UC Davis Stores

A quick option that can use lab accounts directly. Complete the departmental purchase form and send it to Christina for the correct account code.

Urgent / out-of-pocket reimbursement

  1. Get written approval from Christina in advance (email or Slack).
  2. Preferred: ask Christina to purchase with the lab P-Card.
  3. If you must use your own money, keep your receipts — without them we cannot reimburse you.
  4. Complete the Expense Reimbursement form and email it to Christina for an approval signature before continuing. Multiple purchases can go on one form, but the total cannot exceed $500.
  5. Create a Payment/Refund order in pre-purchasing (delivery = your address; shipping = “No Shipping Required”).
  6. In AggieExpense, add the lab manager as an expense delegate (ask Christina for the current manager’s address), with “Can Prepare” and “Can view receipts” checked.
  7. Attach the receipt and completed reimbursement form and submit for approval.

Tracking & pickup

All purchase requests and pickups are tracked in Slack (Purchase Hub → Tracker List). When an item arrives it’s posted in #purchasing, and whoever picks it up logs where it was stored.
